column1: # ssim2-gpu warm_ref VRAM remeasurement — task138 Part 1
# base_commit=e9aa4426f7b6 host=lilith gpu=NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 driver=596.21 date=2026-05-28
# command: WORKER_MODE=<mode> WORKER_W=<w> WORKER_H=<h> WORKER_REPS=<reps> target/release/examples/mem_one_size
# Protocol: subprocess-per-cell, nvidia-smi memory.used polled tightly during run, peak-baseline delta.
# CUDA backend. reps column = WORKER_REPS. OOM rows: pool grew past card ceiling (~11.9GiB used) and CUDA_ERROR_OUT_OF_MEMORY.
# KEY FINDING: ssim2-gpu whole-image warm_ref shares the IDENTICAL 57-plane/scale Scale buffer set with full
# (Ssim2::new). No transient ref scratch exists to free. Steady-state peak is identical to full at every
# measurable size; the published TSV "warm_ref > full" gap is a cubecl-pool-growth-vs-sampling-window artifact.
# --- reps=2 variance trials at 16mp (smoking gun: under the published protocol the which-mode-is-higher is NOISE ~+-60MiB) ---

column14: reps2-trial2 (full HIGHER than warm_ref — opposite of published 'regression')
